servinfo.title: Discharge Headlight Description
servinfo.ptxt: The discharge headlight consists of a discharge headlight bulb, a ballast and an igniter.

servinfosub.title: Discharge Headlight Bulb
servinfosub.ptxt: The discharge headlight bulb is used as the high beam and low beam light. The bulb contains xenon gas (1), mercury (3) and metal iodide (2), which emit light by means of arc-discharge created between tungsten electrodes (4) inside the bulb.

servinfosub.title: Ballast
servinfosub.ptxt: The ballast boosts the voltage from the battery, converts the current from DC to AC and sends it to the igniter. It also controls the voltage necessary for stable and continuous lighting. When an abnormality is detected, it initiates fail-safe control.

servinfosub.title: Igniter
servinfosub.ptxt: When the increased voltage from the ballast is charged in the igniter circuit and rises to a certain voltage level, the igniter applies it to the bulb.
